decisions.title: Owner ratifies the 14-clause source-to-runtime ownership contract
decisions.decision: All 14 non-negotiable target-state clauses in yazelix_runtime_convergence/PLAN.md are ratified as-is. This clears the YZXCONV-002 human-approval gate and unblocks Waves 1-3. Ratification is authority to design and build to the contract; each mutating step (profile closure, agent config, branch publication) still requires its own per-step owner approval and rollback receipt. No blanket apply authority is granted.

decisions.title: Strict-Nushell routing on both agent shell tools, wrapper-lane convergence as documented fallback
decisions.decision: Clause 11 (agents execute shell only through Nushell) targets strict enforcement on BOTH agents: (a) Claude's PreToolUse Bash hook (rtk hook claude) is extended to rewrite every command to `nu -c` through the single rtk_wrappers.nu chokepoint; (b) the Codex codex-harness-hook is extended to rewrite Bash exec to `nu -c`. Where the Codex `bash -lc` exec floor cannot be forced, the documented FALLBACK is wrapper-lane convergence: every agent command still routes through the single RTK/Nushell wrapper, and the bash-exec-wrapping is recorded as the platform floor rather than treated as an open gap. No unverified shell-override claim may be made; the codex-harness-hook rewrite feasibility must be verified against the installed Codex build before implementation (YZXCONV-026).
evidence: docs.claude.com hooks reference: a PreToolUse Bash hook honors hookSpecificOutput.updatedInput.command to substitute the executed command
openai/codex config reference: no config key selects a custom shell; [shell_environment_policy] shapes the subprocess environment only, not the interpreter
openai/codex issues #1708 and #6715: the exec layer force-wraps commands in bash -lc even under experimental_use_profile
